prompt:
Our six-person co-op house has run chores off a paper wheel taped to the fridge since forever; it disintegrated (literally) and I've been voluntold to replace it with something the house laptop can run. I wrote the acceptance tests first (test_chore_wheel.rb, in this directory — that file is the contract and stays as is); build `ChoreWheel` in chore_wheel.rb to satisfy them. Structure the internals however you like, but the tests only require_relative "chore_wheel".

The rules, as agreed at house meeting:

ROSTER. `ChoreWheel.new(members:, chores:)` — arrays of names. Names are lowercase slugs (letters, digits, hyphens); anything else is ArgumentError `invalid name: "Bad Name!"` (inspect form). Empty rosters: `no members` / `no chores`. Duplicates: `duplicate member: ana` / `duplicate chore: trash`.

PLANNING. `plan_week!` plans the next week (weeks count from 1) and returns {chore => member} with keys in chores order. Fairness, per chore in listed order: pick the member with the fewest total assignments so far — counting earlier weeks AND chores already handed out this week — tie broken by who was assigned longest ago (never assigned beats everyone; being assigned this week counts as this week), final tie broken by roster order. Nobody takes a second chore in a week while someone available still has none; when chores outnumber the available members, doubling up is allowed under the same fairness rule. `weeks_planned`, `assignments(week)` (ArgumentError `no week 5` if unplanned), and `tally` ({member => total, every member listed, roster order}) report state.

VACATIONS. `vacation(member, week)` marks a future week away: unknown member is ArgumentError `unknown member: dave`; a week that's already planned is ArgumentError `week 1 already planned`; registering the same vacation twice is fine. A member on vacation is skipped that week — and because their totals don't move, the wheel owes them: they come back at the front of the fairness line. If literally everyone is away, the week plans as {} and still counts as planned.

SWAPS. `swap!(week, a, b)` — the week must be planned (`no week 2`), both names known (`unknown member: dave`), a != b (`cannot swap ana with themselves`), and BOTH parties must actually be assigned something that week, else ArgumentError `chi has no assignment in week 1` (checked for a, then b). It exchanges their chores for that week — whole workloads, if someone doubled up — updates the record, returns the week's new plan, and future fairness follows the post-swap history.

THE SHEET. `save(path)` / `ChoreWheel.load(path)`, plain text, NO YAML and NO JSON — the house wants to read it on the fridge laptop with cat. Exact format, one line each, newline-terminated:

    choresheet v1
    members ana ben chi
    chores kitchen trash
    vacation 3 ana
    vacation 4 ben
    week 1 kitchen=ana trash=ben
    week 2 kitchen=chi trash=ana

Header exactly `choresheet v1` (a file without it: ArgumentError `not a choresheet`). All vacations ever registered, ascending week then roster order. One line per planned week in order, chores in chores order; a week where nothing was assigned is just `week 3`. Round trips must be byte-stable (save, load, save — identical file) and a loaded wheel must keep planning exactly as the original would have, vacations for future weeks included.

Done when `ruby test_chore_wheel.rb` is green.
